summary: Ten concrete recommendations surfaced from 30 fully crawled HN front-page stories.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 30/30 current front-page stories processed | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Book Prize Index
items.source_story_title: Quality non-fiction books are the antithesis of AI slop
items.summary: A searchable nonfiction-book discovery site organized around literary and subject awards.
items.why_included: A reader found strong technology and science picks and said it revived their reading habit.
items.evidence: A reader reported finding excellent books and new book-club ideas after browsing its categories.

evidence_sources.author: titanomachy
evidence_sources.excerpt: I sorted the "technology" and "science" sections and saw a few excellent books that I've read and a few that I would really like to read.

items.name: Inksightful
items.source_story_title: Writing by hand is good for your brain
items.summary: An iOS app that turns photographed handwriting into searchable digital text.
items.why_included: Its maker described using it successfully to digitize handwritten diaries and general paper notes.
items.evidence: The maker says the app worked for handwritten diaries and any handwritten paper.

evidence_sources.author: brian-dewey
evidence_sources.excerpt: I used it to digitize my handwritten diaries, but it works for any handwritten paper.

items.name: The Beam Engine
items.summary: An interactive visual explainer of the steam engine and its mechanical controls.
items.why_included: The technical explainer earned direct praise for making a complex historic machine approachable.
items.evidence: A reader explicitly said they loved the article.

items.name: Pikuma
items.source_story_title: Software rendering in 500 lines of bare C++
items.summary: A course library covering low-level programming, graphics, compilers, and game development.
items.why_included: A learner praised its lectures after working through both compiler and 3D-renderer courses.
items.evidence: A commenter called Gustavo's lectures great and reported using two of the courses.

evidence_sources.author: AlexeyBrin
evidence_sources.excerpt: Gustavo's lectures are great! I work through his Compiler lectures now and I bought the 3D renderer course too.

items.name: LearnOpenGL
items.source_story_title: Learn OpenGL, extensive tutorial resource for learning Modern OpenGL
items.summary: A practical tutorial sequence for learning modern graphics programming with OpenGL.
items.why_included: A commenter strongly endorsed it as a fundamentals-first route into computer graphics.
items.evidence: A commenter urged graphics beginners to work through the site and its examples sequentially.

evidence_sources.author: cyber_kinetist
evidence_sources.excerpt: If you're starting to learn computer graphics, just study through the entire site and do the examples one by one.

items.name: Sokol
items.source_story_title: Learn OpenGL, extensive tutorial resource for learning Modern OpenGL
items.summary: A lightweight cross-platform C framework for graphics, audio, and application development.
items.why_included: It was directly recommended as a practical next step after learning graphics fundamentals.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended Sokol for applying the graphics knowledge gained from LearnOpenGL.

evidence_sources.author: AyanamiKaine
evidence_sources.excerpt: If you want to use the learned knowledge I would recommend to use something like Sokol

items.name: Learn WebGPU for C++
items.summary: A C++ tutorial for WebGPU graphics programming, from setup through rendering fundamentals.
items.why_included: A practitioner called it the best WebGPU tutorial they found after using it in a project.
items.evidence: A commenter said the resource taught them WebGPU and explicitly recommended it.

evidence_sources.author: ChadNauseam
evidence_sources.excerpt: This resource taught me WebGPU a couple years ago. I can recommend it. It's the best WebGPU tutorial I've found.

items.name: Palmier Pro
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Palmier Pro – Open-source macOS video editor built for AI
items.summary: An open-source native macOS video editor built around AI-assisted editing workflows.
items.why_included: Independent commenters praised the project and its promotional demonstration.
items.evidence: A commenter described the editor as very nice and its promo video as wonderful.

evidence_sources.author: draw_down
evidence_sources.excerpt: I don't work with video but this seems very nice, and your promo video is wonderful. Great work!

items.name: OneCLI
items.source_story_title: Show HN: OneCLI – OSS credential gateway that keeps secrets out of AI agents
items.summary: An open-source credential gateway that keeps secrets outside AI-agent execution environments.
items.why_included: The thread positively received its credential-broker pattern for isolating secrets from agents.
items.evidence: A commenter called the project nice and described an analogous GitHub-specific safety proxy.

evidence_sources.author: denysvitali
evidence_sources.excerpt: Nice! I've built something like this, although specific to GitHub

items.name: ModelScope
items.source_story_title: Startup founders urge U.S. government not to shut off Chinese open weight AI
items.summary: A China-based model hub hosting open-weight models, finetunes, and quantized variants.
items.why_included: It was directly suggested as a practical alternative catalog for Chinese open-weight models.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended it as the Chinese Hugging Face equivalent for finding Chinese models.

evidence_sources.author: shixinhb
evidence_sources.excerpt: Instead you can use the Chinese version of huggingface with all Chinese models
